scope.enriched_hint_one: {{count}} target with data for the Attack Map
scope.enriched_hint_other: {{count}} targets with data for the Attack Map
scope.sync_phases_title: Sync scope from phase logs (recon, scan, exploitation)
scope.sync_up_to_date: Scope is already up to date with phase data.
scope.no_items_hint: Add IPs, domains or URLs to the engagement scope.
scope.footer_hint: 💡 Expand each target to record OS, ports and vulnerabilities. Using "Import from Scope" in the Attack Map will automatically load this data.

eng.delete_desc2: All phases, operation logs, findings, evidence and associated documents will be deleted.

engs.title: Engagements
engs.subtitle: {{count}} engagement(s) registered
engs.search_placeholder: Search by title, client or codename...

arsenal.title: Arsenal
arsenal.helpDescription: Command and tool library organized by phase. Use them in engagements to record your operation log.
arsenal.helpTip1: Filter by phase or tool to find commands quickly
arsenal.helpTip2: Use 'Add to engagement' to log a command in the active operation
arsenal.helpTip3: Create custom commands and tools with the add buttons
arsenal.noResultsHint: Try a different term or create a new command.
arsenal.addToOpLog: Select an engagement and log this command in the operation
arsenal.fieldTitlePlaceholder: e.g.: Nmap full scan, SQLMap authentication bypass...
arsenal.toolFieldDescPlaceholder: What is this tool used for?
arsenal.toolDeleteConfirm: Delete tool "{{label}}" and all its commands?

evidence.title: Engagement Evidence
evidence.drop_types: Images, screenshots, scripts, logs - max {{max}} MB
evidence.caption_placeholder: e.g. SQLi screenshot on login, RCE confirmed...

techniques.title: Engagement Techniques
techniques.empty_hint: Add from the MITRE catalog or a manual technique.
techniques.manual_hint: Add a technique, tool or methodology not in the MITRE catalog.
techniques.name_placeholder: e.g. OWASP WSTG - Authentication Testing, ZAP Spider...
techniques.tool_placeholder: e.g. OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite, manual...
techniques.helpDescription: Browser for tactics and techniques from the MITRE ATT&CK framework. Covers the full attack chain: reconnaissance, initial access, escalation, persistence and more.
techniques.helpTip1: Each technique includes tools, detection signals and mitigations
techniques.helpTip2: Click the Terminal icon to see related commands in the Arsenal
techniques.helpTip3: Filter by pentest phase to see relevant techniques for each stage
techniques.helpTip4: The MITRE ID (T1234) links directly to official documentation at attack.mitre.org
techniques.selectHint: Select a technique from the left panel.
techniques.engagementTip: Did you exploit this technique in an engagement? Record the finding with CVSS from the active engagement workspace.

config.title: Report Template
config.subtitle: Firm details for the cover page and footer of PDF reports
config.card_desc: Firm details that appear on the cover page and footer of pentest reports.
config.disclaimer_hint: Appears on the cover page of each generated report.
config.admin_only: Only administrators can modify the settings.

report_tmpl.title: Report Templates
report_tmpl.empty_hint: Create a custom template to export Word reports.
report_tmpl.word_subtitle: Design the structure of your .docx reports with dynamic placeholders
report_tmpl.select_desc: Templates define the structure of exported Word reports. Use placeholders like
report_tmpl.quick_ref_hint: Click any placeholder to insert it into the editor, or use the "Insert placeholder" button in the toolbar.
report_tmpl.help_desc: Design custom HTML templates for Word reports. Insert placeholders that are automatically replaced with engagement data on export.
report_tmpl.help_tip1: Use the "Insert placeholder" button to add variables like {{CLIENT_NAME}}
report_tmpl.help_tip3: Mark a template as "default" for use in Reports
report_tmpl.help_tip4: Edit HTML directly with the "Source code" button in the toolbar

tmpl.title: Templates
tmpl.subtitle: {{count}} template(s) - built-in + custom
tmpl.selectToView: Select a template to view details
tmpl.orCreate: or create a new one with the "New" button

help.title: Finding Templates
help.description: Reusable library of security findings. Includes built-in system templates and custom ones created by the team. Avoid writing the most common findings from scratch.
help.tip1: Create your own templates with the "New" button
help.tip2: When registering a finding in an engagement, you can search and apply a template with one click
help.tip3: Built-in templates cannot be deleted - only admins can modify them

2fa.title: Two-factor authentication
2fa.desc_inactive: Enable two-factor authentication for better security.
2fa.disable_confirm: Are you sure you want to disable 2FA?
2fa.scan: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app.

planning.objectives_label: What is the goal? (scope, expected deliverables)
planning.roe_label: Operation rules agreed with the client
planning.restrictions_label: Systems, ranges or actions explicitly out of scope
planning.contacts_label: Technical and executive contacts during the engagement

recon_phase.complete_fields: Fill in the finding or notes

scanning_phase.complete_fields: Fill in at least target or service

exploit_phase.complete_fields: Fill in the exploit type or payload
exploit_phase.scan_findings_hint: Click a finding to load its target into the exploitation form.
exploit_phase.scan_findings_available: There are {{count}} scanning finding(s) to work with.

post_phase.auth_warning: Post-exploitation only if explicitly authorized in the Rules of Engagement. Any action here must be covered by the signed scope.
post_phase.complete_fields: Fill in the action type or result

reporting_phase.executive_summary_label: For management audience - no technical jargon
reporting_phase.conclusions_label: Technical analysis of the attack vectors found
reporting_phase.observations_label: Controls that work well, observed best practices
reporting_phase.empty: No findings in the engagement yet.
reporting_phase.empty_hint: Complete the previous phases and add findings.

finding.cve_hint: Auto-fills CVSS 3.1, CWE and description from NVD (NIST).
finding.cvss_hint: Select values for each metric to calculate the CVSS 3.1 base score. The vector is synced automatically on save.
finding.delete_confirm: Delete this finding? This action cannot be undone.

client.crm_empty_hint: Only clients with budgets in accepted, in-progress, or renewed status that include pentesting or offensive security services are imported.
client.delete_confirm: Delete client "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

users.delete_confirm: Delete user "{{username}}"? This action cannot be undone.

ops.title: Operations Dashboard
ops.subtitle: Executive metrics - last 6 months
ops.error_loading: Error loading data. Check the backend connection.
ops.owasp_empty: No data. Findings with OWASP categories will appear here.

reports.title: Reports
reports.subtitle: Generate technical and executive PDF reports
reports.selectEngagementCta: Select an engagement to generate the report
reports.selectEngagementHint: The report will include all findings and phases of the selected engagement.
reports.needFinding: The engagement needs at least one finding to generate a report
reports.optScopeHint: Adds an appendix with the defined engagement scope
reports.optPhasesHint: Adds operation logs for each phase
reports.pdfDescription: Complete pentest report with executive summary, findings, CVSS and recommendations.
reports.wordDescription: Custom editable report using the configured template.
reports.logoHint: Logo appears on the cover page and header of the report.
reports.confidentialityNote: This report is confidential and intended solely for the client.

help.title: Reports
help.description: Generate technical and executive PDF reports from completed engagements. Customize the template, logo and sections to include.
help.tip1: Select an engagement and configure the options before generating
help.tip2: Use a custom logo for client deliverables
help.tip3: The Word template is useful for making edits before final delivery
help.tip4: Only open findings are included by default - change with the 'Only open' option

scripts.title: Scripts
scripts.subtitle: Offensive security scripts library
scripts.authorizationNote: For authorized use only. Always obtain written authorization before running offensive scripts.

help.title: Scripts
help.description: Custom and built-in offensive security scripts. Filter by phase, language or platform.
help.tip3: Download scripts directly or copy to clipboard

writeups.title: Writeups
writeups.subtitle: CTF, HTB, TryHackMe and Bug Bounty writeups
writeups.importHint: Imports title, scope and findings from the engagement as a starting point.
writeups.validationBugBounty: At least a program or vulnerability URL is required
writeups.sectionSummaryHint: General writeup summary for this machine / challenge
writeups.sectionEnumerationHint: Initial enumeration: ports, services, web discovery
writeups.sectionServiceEnumHint: Service enumeration: banners, versions, manual probing
writeups.sectionPostExploitHint: Post-exploitation: persistence, lateral movement, data collection
writeups.impactPlaceholder: RCE, data exfiltration, full system compromise...
writeups.sectionLabSummaryHint: Summary of your OSCP lab progress
writeups.cvssHint: Enter CVSS vector to calculate base score automatically
writeups.ctaPdfDescription: Generates a professional PDF document with cover page and structured content.
writeups.privacyNote: Writeups are private - only visible to your account.

help.title: Writeups
help.description: Document CTF, HTB, TryHackMe, OSCP and Bug Bounty writeups. Export to PDF with professional formatting.
help.tip1: Select the platform type (HTB, THM, CTF, Bug Bounty, OSCP) to auto-adapt the form
help.tip2: Import from an engagement to pre-fill scope and findings
help.tip3: Use the Markdown editor to format content with code blocks, links and lists
help.tip4: Export to PDF for professional documentation or portfolio

biblioteca.title: Finding Templates Library
biblioteca.subtitle: Reference finding templates by category and severity
biblioteca.helpDescription: Curated and custom finding templates. Use them as a starting point when adding findings to engagements.
biblioteca.helpTip1: Use 'Import to engagement' to add a template as a finding directly
biblioteca.helpTip2: Create custom templates with the 'New' button
biblioteca.helpTip3: Built-in curated templates cannot be deleted
biblioteca.emptyState: Select a template to view details
biblioteca.emptyStateHint: Or create a new one with the 'New' button
biblioteca.importTip: Importing creates a finding in the active engagement with this template's data pre-filled.

papers.title: Papers
papers.subtitle: Security research papers and CVEs
papers.helpDescription: Security research paper editor. Generate PDFs with academic structure for conferences (USENIX, Black Hat, EkoParty, Exploit-DB, etc.) or technical publications.
papers.helpTip1: Select the target conference to automatically adapt the format
papers.helpTip2: Fill in the sections in the left panel and generate the PDF from the right panel
papers.helpTip3: The 'Exploit-DB' tab lets you search and save papers as reference
papers.helpTip4: Saved papers stay in your account and can be edited later
papers.headerSubtitle: Document security research for conferences and publication
papers.bannerNoTitle: Write the title in the left panel
papers.editorNote: Follow responsible disclosure before publishing vulnerability research. Black Hat states that LLM-generated content cannot be the main body of a submission - use this tool to structure and format, but the research must be yours.
papers.edbHeaderDesc: Search papers published on exploit-db.com - download them or save them to your library
papers.edbSearchPromptHint: Enter a term above and press Search
papers.edbNoSavedHint: Search papers in the 'Search' tab and save them to have them handy
papers.edbFooterNote: Exploit-DB is a public security repository maintained by Offensive Security. Saved papers are stored locally in Gungnir for quick reference. Respect the terms of use and authorship of the papers you download.
papers.edbNoPdf: This entry has no PDF - it is code or text. Use the EDB button to view it.
papers.sec01Hint: Brief summary of the research, vulnerability, and conclusions
papers.sec03Hint: Technical background, concepts, and related work
papers.sec04Hint: Threat actors, attack surface, and assumptions
papers.sec05Hint: Research approach, tools, and techniques used
papers.sec06Hint: Detailed technical description of the vulnerability found
papers.sec07Hint: Analysis of the underlying cause of the vulnerability
papers.sec09Hint: Exploit code, screenshots, logs proving exploitability
papers.sec10Hint: CVSS score, justification, and severity classification
papers.sec11Hint: Recommended patches, workarounds, and defensive measures
papers.sec12Hint: Responsible disclosure process, timeline, and authorization
papers.sec13Hint: Summary, lessons learned, and future research directions
papers.sec14Hint: CVEs, advisories, papers, and related resources
papers.sec15Hint: Dates of discovery, vendor contact, fix, and public disclosure
papers.sec16Hint: Additional data, scripts, configurations, or supporting material

engform.desc_external_pt: External network, perimeter, DMZ, VPNs, internet-exposed services
engform.desc_internal_pt: From the internal network, Active Directory, lateral movement, privilege escalation
engform.desc_web_app: OWASP Top 10, authentication, authorization, business logic, injections
engform.desc_api: REST, GraphQL, SOAP - authentication, authorization, rate-limiting, injection
engform.desc_mobile: Android / iOS - OWASP MASVS, static and dynamic analysis, SSL traffic
engform.desc_red_team: Advanced adversary simulation (APT) with defined business objectives
engform.desc_social_eng: Phishing, vishing, spear-phishing, pretexting - targeted campaign simulation
engform.desc_physical: Physical security, tailgating, credential cloning, facility access
engform.desc_app_security: SAST + DAST + manual review - complete application security assessment
engform.desc_code_review: Security-focused source code review: insecure patterns, dependencies, secrets
engform.desc_arch_review: Threat modeling, architecture review, attack surface, design controls
engform.desc_security_audit: Technical review of controls: configurations, hardening, access, patches, FW rules
engform.desc_incident_response: Active incident management: detection, containment, eradication, forensics, recovery
engform.desc_preliminary: Scoping and reconnaissance prior to an offensive engagement - no active exploitation
engform.mode_pentesting_desc: Fixed phases: Planning - Reconnaissance - Scanning - Exploitation - Post-exploitation - Reporting
engform.mode_custom_desc: Phases and stages defined by you. Attach documents, work plans and updates.
engform.mode_pentesting_info: 6 fixed phases with specialized tools: Scope, Attack Map, Arsenal, Scanner Import, MITRE Techniques, Findings with CVSS.
engform.mode_custom_info: Phases and stages defined by you. Attach documents, work plans and record updates at each stage.
engform.custom_phases_info: This type uses customizable phases with documents, work plans and updates per stage.
engform.template_phases_intro: The following phases will be created automatically:
engform.phases_rename_hint: You can rename, reorder or add phases after creating the engagement.

cvss.av_adjacent_desc: Requires access to the same local network
cvss.av_local_desc: Requires local access to the system
cvss.av_physical_desc: Requires physical access to the hardware
cvss.ui_none_desc: No action from any user required
cvss.ui_required_desc: Requires a user to take some action
cvss.scope_unchanged_desc: Impact is limited to the vulnerable component
